title: aita for wanting to quit my job when i'm the only employee?
text: i just got hired a few weeks ago for a small business and i was told very vaguely about what i would be doing. i said "yeah, no problem, i can do all of that easy." well, since i was trained and then left on my own, i've realized there's a lot more to my job than what i was originally told. i'm essentially the one running the business and i work under the owner. this is my first job out of college, i have a marketing degree, and i legitimately have no idea what i'm doing as far as explaining to customers about tinting. i have no passion for tinting or cars, and i find myself dreading to go to work and hating my 9 hour shift all 5 days of the week. i'm the only employee, and he only hired me because i said i wouldn't leave right away after getting the job. he's been struggling to hire someone who will stay longer than 6 months, and here i am wanting to quit after 4 weeks. i really really don't want to make his life harder than it already is, but this is already taking a toll on my mental health and my happiness. don't get me wrong, he's a very nice guy, but this job is just not cut out for me. am i the asshole for wanting to quit my job when i'm the only employee and i'm the one basically running the business?

storyboard.content: I just got hired a few weeks ago at a small business. I was told very vaguely about what I would be doing, and I said no problem.
This is my first job out of college. I have a marketing degree, and I legitimately have no idea what I'm doing when it comes to explaining tinting to customers.
Since I was trained and left on my own, I've realized there's a lot more to the job than what I was originally told. I'm essentially the one running the entire business under the owner.
I want to quit after only 4 weeks, even though I told the owner I wouldn't leave right away when I was hired.
I have no passion for tinting or cars, and I find myself dreading going to work and hating my 9-hour shifts 5 days a week. It's taking a toll on my mental health and happiness.
I'm the only employee. The owner has been struggling to hire someone who will stay longer than 6 months.
I really don't want to make his life harder than it already is. He's a very nice guy, but this job is just not cut out for me.
